title: The Chronicle attributed to Robert of Gloucester
summary: The pinned General Corpus XML copies U-M's 2006 uncorrected OCR of William Aldis Wright's 1887 two-volume edition. Wright principally follows Cotton Caligula A XI, uses a Brock working transcript as an aid, supplies a 152-line Cotton lacuna from Harley 201, and collates eight named sigla across longer- and shorter-recension evidence. Separate entities preserve the U-M historical page-image layer, a transformed current Medictionary successor encoding, the two Wright volume expressions, their two independent Internet Archive scan carriers, the physical witnesses, Wright's 1868 collation notebook, a complete provider-exposed Trinity facsimile, and a one-canvas Digby selection. Access and rights remain component-specific.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for ahb1378
entities.description: The exact checked-out CME XML artifact at the pinned submodule commit. Its cataloged work is the one Chronicle represented in the BODY text and appendix divisions; correction divisions and omitted-matter placeholders are editorial/digital representation layers rather than additional works.
notes: The XML root is DLPSTEXTCLASS rather than namespace-qualified TEI.
The raw XML has 61 DIV2 representation sections, 197 LG, 13,683 L, and 12,637 NOTE1; raw L is not canonical lineation.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item AHB1378.0001.001
entities.description: The U-M digital text whose header dates creation to 2006, identifies uncorrected OCR and TEI in Libraries Level 1 encoding, and historically states that digital page images were linked.
notes: The generic 2006 availability warning and current U-M rights statement are preserved as distinct time layers.
The header's linked-image statement is not treated as proof that an item-level downloadable image package remains currently accessible.

entities.label: U-M page-image layer for AHB1378.0001.001
entities.description: The historical page-image layer referenced by the 2006 U-M XML through PB/EPB milestones and described in its header as linked 600 dpi TIFF G4 images. The image sequence represents both Wright volumes, but the scanned physical copies, complete inventory, and present complete delivery route were not verified.
entities.reproduction_format: Historically linked 600 dpi TIFF G4 page images represented by 1,112 PB and 1,113 EPB milestones in the pinned XML
notes: This is a distinct U-M scan-delivery layer and is not identified with either independently cached Internet Archive scan carrier.
The XML establishes a historical page-image sequence, not an auditable current complete bundle or exhaustive physical-surface inventory.

entities.label: Medictionary XML for ahb1378
entities.description: A current public successor XML derived from the U-M CME item but serialized in an ETS/EEBO-style structure. It retains the same 13,683 L, 197 LG, 61 DIV2, and 1,112 PB counts while normalizing element names, removing EPB elements, restructuring front matter, changing availability text, and modifying other markup.
entities.reproduction_format: ETS/EEBO-style XML successor encoding derived from the U-M CME item
notes: The Medictionary XML is not byte-identical or canonical-XML-identical to the pinned CME XML and is modeled as a separate digital encoding.
Equivalent aggregate L/LG/DIV2/PB counts do not establish complete semantic or textual identity.

entities.label: Abstract Chronicle attributed to Robert of Gloucester
entities.description: Schema-compromise conceptual entity for the one medieval intellectual work shared by the longer and shorter recensions. It is not a physical catalog record, edition, or digital artifact; catalog_description is the closest available non-carrier entity type.
notes: The paired metadata manifest counts this abstract Chronicle once.
The entity exists to keep intellectual-work identity distinct from Wright's edition and the repository artifact.

entities.label: Longer recension of the Chronicle attributed to Robert of Gloucester
entities.description: Schema-compromise conceptual entity for the longer recension, represented in Wright's main stream principally from Cotton Caligula A XI. It is a version layer, not a second cataloged work or a physical carrier.
notes: The internal date remains in tension with the British Library's last-quarter-thirteenth-century date for Cotton Caligula A XI.

entities.label: Shorter recension of the Chronicle attributed to Robert of Gloucester
entities.description: Schema-compromise conceptual entity for the shorter recension, represented in Wright's Greek-siglum readings and forty-eight appendix blocks. It is a version layer, not a second cataloged work or a physical carrier.

entities.label: Wright's 1887 edition of The Metrical Chronicle of Robert of Gloucester
entities.description: The two-volume scholarly edition project that prints a principally Cotton-based longer-recension main stream, a Harley lacuna supply, extensive collation notes, shorter-recension appendix blocks, and corrections.

bibliographic.citation: William Aldis Wright, ed., The Metrical Chronicle of Robert of Gloucester, Rolls Series 86, 2 vols. (London: printed for Her Majesty's Stationery Office by Eyre and Spottiswoode, 1887).
bibliographic.title: The Metrical Chronicle of Robert of Gloucester
bibliographic.publisher: Her Majesty's Stationery Office; printed by Eyre and Spottiswoode

entities.label: Wright 1887 edition, volume I
entities.description: The first volume expression, containing Wright's preface and the main Chronicle through printed p. 494.

bibliographic.citation: William Aldis Wright, ed., The Metrical Chronicle of Robert of Gloucester, vol. I, Rolls Series 86 (London: HMSO, 1887).
bibliographic.title: The Metrical Chronicle of Robert of Gloucester
bibliographic.publisher: Her Majesty's Stationery Office; printed by Eyre and Spottiswoode

entities.label: Wright 1887 edition, volume II
entities.description: The second volume expression, containing the main Chronicle from printed p. 495 through p. 776, shorter-recension appendix blocks at pp. 780–877, glossary/index outside the cataloged work, and corrections at pp. 1010–1017.

bibliographic.citation: William Aldis Wright, ed., The Metrical Chronicle of Robert of Gloucester, vol. II, Rolls Series 86 (London: HMSO, 1887).
bibliographic.title: The Metrical Chronicle of Robert of Gloucester
bibliographic.publisher: Her Majesty's Stationery Office; printed by Eyre and Spottiswoode

entities.label: E. Brock's working transcript of Cotton Caligula A XI
entities.description: A modern working transcript prepared for Wright's edition. It is a process intermediary, not a ninth medieval witness and not the sole route by which Wright knew Cotton.
notes: Wright calls Brock's transcript admirable and acknowledges substantial help from it.
No present shelfmark, repository, or survival evidence was verified.

entities.label: Wright's 1868 Robert of Gloucester collation notebook
entities.description: Trinity College Cambridge Add. MS c/172, an archival process document recording collation of Cotton and Digby and readings from Trinity R.4.26 and CUL Ee.4.31. It supports the edition's working history but is not modeled as a textual ancestor.
notes: Modern handwritten process carrier, not a medieval textual witness. manuscript_witness is a provisional schema compromise because repository_artifact is reserved for project-held artifacts.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Caligula A XI
entities.description: Wright's principal longer-recension witness. The Chronicle occupies ff. 3r–13v and 15r–168v and is imperfect; two leaves after f. 74 are missing.

date_statements.label: Current repository date
date_statements.value: Last quarter of the thirteenth century
date_statements.notes: This catalog date remains in tension with scholarship's internal post-1297 and possible post-1307 dating of the longer recension.

holding.notes: Exact Chronicle component record; parent record 040-001102340.

physical_description.extent: Chronicle ff. 3r–13v and 15r–168v within a composite codex
notes: Imperfect and fire-damaged; two leaves are missing after f. 74.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 201
entities.description: A longer-recension witness at ff. 4r–161r. Wright uses it to supply 152 lines after line 4920 where Cotton lacks two leaves and rechecked its quoted readings directly.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 19677
entities.description: An imperfect longer-recension witness used by Wright for the range corresponding to his lines 4684–11813. Four leaves are missing at the beginning.

physical_description.extent: Chronicle begins at f. 1 after four missing leaves and ends before a brief chronicle at f. 92v

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS R.4.26
entities.description: A shorter-recension witness whose Chronicle occupies ff. 2v–163r. A Middle French prose Brut also occupies ff. 159v–163r, so the closing surfaces are shared.

notes: The 170-leaf extent reconciles to four front flyleaves plus 166 numbered leaves in the IIIF sequence.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Digby 205
entities.description: A shorter-recension witness. The current medieval catalog dates it to the fourteenth century, while the separate one-canvas Digital Bodleian manifest says mid-fifteenth century; both claims are preserved.

date_statements.label: Digital Bodleian selection metadata
date_statements.notes: Provider records disagree; the dates are not silently harmonized.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ee.4.31
entities.description: A shorter-recension witness whose Chronicle occupies ff. 52r–199v and is abridged after the Conquest. Current discovery relies on the digitized nineteenth-century catalog rather than a verified item-level CUDL object.

date_statements.label: CUL printed catalog date
date_statements.value: Latter part of the fifteenth century

date_statements.label: Wright's estimate
date_statements.notes: Wright also describes vellum, while the catalog describes a chiefly parchment codex with paper and parchment.

physical_description.support: paper and parchment; chiefly parchment in the printed catalog
notes: Wright describes vellum and a hand change after f. 83; the disagreement is retained.

entities.label: Senate House Library MS 278
entities.description: The current identity of Wright's Mostyn witness, not an additional manuscript. It contains a shorter-recension verse Chronicle ending at 1272.

physical_description.extent: 149 leaves; Chronicle ff. 1–146 with Latin material at f. 147

notes: Physical access is currently closed for conservation.

entities.label: Magdalene College Pepys Library MS 2014
entities.description: A defective shorter-recension fragment within a parchment miscellany. Wright records portions corresponding to lines 4216–5811, Appendix II lines 1–18, lines 8557–9137, and Appendix XX.

date_statements.label: Specialist catalog date
date_statements.value: End of the fourteenth or beginning of the fifteenth century

entities.label: Internet Archive scan carrier for Wright 1887 volume I
entities.description: The complete Internet Archive digital scan object for the first printed volume, kept separate from the Wright volume expression.
entities.reproduction_format: 600-source-page digitization; 596-page text PDF plus metadata and scandata

entities.label: Internet Archive scan carrier for Wright 1887 volume II
entities.description: The complete Internet Archive digital scan object for the second printed volume, kept separate from the Wright volume expression.
entities.reproduction_format: 580-source-page digitization; 576-page text PDF plus metadata and scandata

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge complete provider-exposed IIIF object for MS R.4.26
entities.description: The complete 344-canvas provider manifest inventory: front and back covers, board interiors, four front flyleaves, and ff. 1r–166v. No spine or edge views are exposed, so completeness is bounded to the published IIIF object.
entities.reproduction_format: 344-canvas IIIF Presentation 2 sequence cached as 344 JPEGs at 1800 × 2371 plus manifest and exact-source inventory
notes: The Chronicle occupies canvases 14–335 / ff. 2v–163r; the closing surfaces overlap a French prose Brut from f. 159v.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian one-canvas object for MS Digby 205
entities.description: The complete published one-canvas provider object, containing only f. 9r. It includes Chronicle text but is partial manuscript coverage and never stands for a complete Digby facsimile.
entities.reproduction_format: one-canvas IIIF Presentation object cached as one 1800 × 2755 JPEG plus manifest and inventory

entities.label: Matthew Fisher's Once Called Albion thesis
entities.description: A specialist study used for attribution, recension, date, dialect-region, and witness-transmission evidence; it is contextual scholarship, not a textual ancestor.

bibliographic.citation: Matthew Fisher, Once Called Albion: The Composition and Transmission of History Writing in England, 1280–1350 (DPhil thesis, University of Oxford, 2005).
bibliographic.title: Once Called Albion: The Composition and Transmission of History Writing in England, 1280–1350

object.description: U-M CME digital text represented by the pinned XML

scope.notes: The successor preserves the aggregate 13,683 L, 197 LG, 61 DIV2, and 1,112 PB counts. After excluding NOTE1/NOTE apparatus, the whitespace-normalized direct BODY reading stream is identical in both files (SHA-256 3e94574eb4cecbbfdb6e34b800de2a366f5e5813d9531e097345184273935053). The full BODY stream including apparatus is not identical: the successor changes the root vocabulary, element names and attributes, front-matter structure, availability text, milestone representation, boundary whitespace, and apparatus content such as a pinned GAP element rendered as literal ɯ.

subject.description: volume-I title page, omitted-preface placeholders, and main text through printed p. 494

object.description: main text pp. 495–776, appendix pp. 780–877, source pages represented by placeholders, and corrections pp. 1010–1017

subject.description: quoted B readings, 152-line supply, notes, and corrections

assertion.notes: Wright says that after initially trusting Hearne, he rechecked every B quotation directly.

Greek-siglum β readings and shorter-recension appendix presentation
Greek-siglum γ readings; readings closely follow β and are often not printed separately

rights.component: Pinned repository XML serialization, including repository additions and inherited U-M transcription and markup
rights.jurisdiction: Repository license assertion; inherited-source scope unresolved
rights.basis: The pinned root LICENSE uses Unlicense wording expressly framed for software; no path-specific statement was found that unambiguously extends it to inherited XML data, transcription, or markup.
notes: No repository license is transferred upstream to the medieval work, Wright edition, U-M images, witnesses, or independent scan carriers.

rights.component: U-M digital item AHB1378.0001.001 and its electronic OCR/encoded text, excluding generic site interface assets
rights.basis: U-M's current item record states that the item materials are in the public domain and applies No Copyright–United States to the electronic-text item. This component statement is deliberately limited to AHB1378.0001.001 and does not classify generic site chrome, software, or interface assets.
notes: No Copyright–United States is jurisdiction-specific and is not a worldwide license.
The 2006 XML retains generic 'where applicable' availability wording as a historical statement; it is not promoted here into a definite item-specific restriction.

rights.component: Historical U-M page-image delivery layer for Wright's 1887 edition
rights.basis: The current U-M item applies its public-domain and No Copyright–United States statement to the materials, though complete current image delivery was not verified.
notes: This does not establish a worldwide license, a stable complete TIFF package, or identity with the Internet Archive scan carriers.

rights.component: U-M-derived electronic text represented in the Medictionary XML, including its medieval Chronicle and 1887 source-edition reading layer but excluding Medictionary-specific markup
rights.basis: The verified reading stream derives from U-M item AHB1378.0001.001, whose current item record applies No Copyright–United States to the electronic text; the underlying medieval work and Wright's 1887 publication are independently public domain in the United States. This component statement does not cover Medictionary-specific serialization, transformations, metadata, or markup.
notes: The generic 2006 'where applicable' availability wording remains historical evidence and is not treated as a definite restriction on this item-derived electronic-text component.
Medictionary-specific markup remains separately recorded as unknown rather than inheriting U-M's item statement by implication.

rights.component: Medictionary-specific XML serialization, structural transformations, metadata, and markup
rights.basis: No item-level or site-level license governing the successor XML markup was found during the dated access check; public-domain status of the underlying text is not transferred to new markup by inference.

rights.component: Medieval intellectual work
rights.basis: The work was composed in the Middle Ages and is outside copyright protection in the United States; this says nothing about modern editions, markup, images, or scholarship.

rights.component: Medieval longer recension as an abstract textual version
rights.basis: The recension is medieval and outside copyright protection in the United States; modern editions and encodings remain separate components.

rights.component: Medieval shorter recension as an abstract textual version
rights.basis: The recension is medieval and outside copyright protection in the United States; modern editions and encodings remain separate components.

notes: Status outside the United States is not asserted; independent scan-file and provider terms are separate.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Eight collated witnesses in longer and shorter recensions, with Cotton Caligula A XI as the principal longer-recension base
editorial_practices.representation: Cotton-based main stream, quoted sigla readings, shorter-recension appendix blocks, and later correction apparatus
editorial_practices.description: Wright follows Cotton closely while correcting what he judged obvious errors, uses readings from A/B/C/α/β/γ/δ/ε, and reports that he eventually checked every quoted Harley B reading directly. Add. 18631 and Hunterian U.2.5 are discussed but explicitly not collated and are not promoted into the eight-witness edition graph.
editorial_practices.scope: Both 1887 volumes: main text, notes, appendix, and corrections

editorial_practices.source_feature: Two leaves missing from Cotton Caligula A XI after f. 74
editorial_practices.representation: A locally numbered 152-line passage supplied from Harley 201 after Wright line 4920
editorial_practices.description: The Harley passage is a bounded lacuna supply inside Wright's longer-recension main stream, not a second work or an unqualified change of base witness. The raw XML L total therefore does not equal a single canonical Wright line range.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Shorter-recension additions and alternatives across α, β, γ, δ, and ε
editorial_practices.representation: Forty-eight editorial appendix blocks A–XX keyed to positions in the main stream
editorial_practices.description: The appendix blocks are apparatus/version locators within one Chronicle. They do not establish forty-eight independent poems or works, and Pepys 2014 contributes only bounded surviving passages.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Wright's two-volume edition includes front matter, preface, main text, shorter-recension appendix, glossary/index, corrections, and back matter
editorial_practices.representation: Uncorrected OCR and Level-1 TEI-in-Libraries encoding for the main and appendix streams, with omitted-matter containers and page milestones for untranscribed sections
editorial_practices.description: The U-M header expressly says the OCR was left uncorrected and not content-edited. The encoding retains page sequence while omitting much edition front/back matter and does not turn correction divisions or placeholders into independent work content.

editorial_practices.source_feature: U-M DLPSTEXTCLASS XML with HI1, NOTE1/NOTE2, EPB/PB milestones, omitted containers, and legacy availability wording
editorial_practices.representation: ETS/EEBO-style successor XML with HI/NOTE normalization, EPB removal, PB attribute reduction, front-matter DIV1 restructuring, changed availability metadata, and apparatus-level transformations
editorial_practices.description: The successor is a transformed version, not a byte-identical mirror. Excluding NOTE1/NOTE apparatus, the whitespace-normalized direct BODY reading stream is identical in both files (SHA-256 3e94574eb4cecbbfdb6e34b800de2a366f5e5813d9531e097345184273935053). Including apparatus, the BODY streams differ: one pinned GAP is rendered as literal ɯ, boundary whitespace changes, and one additional descriptive NOTE appears, alongside systematic wrapper and attribute changes.
editorial_practices.scope: Complete current Medictionary XML compared with pinned CME XML e1d77c88

evidence.citation: Pinned CME XML for U-M item AHB1378.0001.001
evidence.locator: FILEDESC, ENCODINGDESC, PROFILEDESC, and complete TEXT; pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c and blob de5d0c7d6b51d26b5176fabd3de68f910ff312e4
evidence.summary: Supports the exact repository artifact, U-M identifier and 2006 header, Wright source-edition citation, uncorrected-OCR practice, historical linked-image layer, main/appendix/corrections boundaries, and legacy availability wording.

evidence.citation: Manual and XPath inspection of the pinned ahb1378 XML
evidence.locator: Counts and boundary inspection for DIV1, DIV2, HEAD, LG, L, NOTE1, NOTE2, P, PB, and EPB; main, appendix, corrections, and omitted containers audited separately
evidence.summary: Confirms 8 DIV1, 61 DIV2, 53 HEAD, 197 LG, 13,683 L, 12,637 NOTE1, 5 NOTE2, 570 P, 1,112 PB, and 1,113 EPB. The main stream has 13 DIV2/194 LG/12,203 L; the forty-eight-block appendix has 48 DIV2/3 LG/1,480 L. Corrections have 325 P/7 PB, and omitted-matter carriers have 223 PB. Raw L is representation extent, not canonical lineation.

evidence.citation: CME root LICENSE at pinned commit e1d77c8
evidence.summary: Provides Unlicense wording expressly framed as a grant for software; no path-specific statement unambiguously extends it to inherited XML data, transcription, or markup.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, CME item AHB1378.0001.001
evidence.locator: Item information, contents, electronic-text statement, and page-linked presentation
evidence.summary: Confirms the current public U-M text, persistent identifier, Wright source citation, section delivery, and current item-level public-domain presentation.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection information and rights
evidence.summary: Provides current collection and rights context, including public-domain/No Copyright–United States presentation, while the 2006 item XML retains older generic restriction wording.

evidence.citation: Dated check of U-M page-image delivery for AHB1378.0001.001
evidence.locator: Current item page links compared with PB/EPB milestones and image statements in the pinned XML
evidence.summary: The XML and current interface establish a historical page-linked image layer, but no stable complete image inventory or bundle, exhaustive physical-surface coverage statement, or scanned-copy identity was verified. No same-as relation to the Internet Archive carriers is supported.

evidence.citation: Medictionary current XML for ahb1378
evidence.summary: Confirms a live successor encoding with ETS/EEBO-style wrappers and the same aggregate L/LG/DIV2/PB counts as the pinned U-M-derived XML, but a different byte stream, canonical XML, metadata layer, and apparatus representation.

evidence.citation: Element-count, normalized-text, attribute, and canonical-XML comparison of the pinned CME and current Medictionary ahb1378 files
evidence.summary: Both files have 13,683 L, 197 LG, 61 DIV2, and 1,112 PB. Excluding NOTE1/NOTE apparatus, their whitespace-normalized direct BODY reading stream is identical (SHA-256 3e94574eb4cecbbfdb6e34b800de2a366f5e5813d9531e097345184273935053), and all 13,683 verse readings match in sequence. Including apparatus, BODY text differs: the successor has 12,643 NOTE versus 12,637 NOTE1 plus 5 NOTE2, adds a descriptive NOTE, turns at least one pinned GAP into literal ɯ, changes boundary whitespace, removes 1,113 EPB, strips non-REF/N PB attributes, normalizes HI1/NOTE1/NOTE2 names, restructures title-page DIV1s, and replaces availability metadata.
notes: Pinned comparison file SHA-256: e6f554fced128b220c70eda70caf2b092bf12e90da2b8843479e4972d739674c.

evidence.citation: William Aldis Wright, ed., The Metrical Chronicle of Robert of Gloucester, vol. I, Rolls Series 86 (1887)
evidence.locator: Title page, preface, and printed pp. 1–494
evidence.summary: Supports volume-I bibliographic data, Wright's editorial statement and witness method, and the longer-recension main text through p. 494.

evidence.citation: William Aldis Wright, ed., The Metrical Chronicle of Robert of Gloucester, vol. II, Rolls Series 86 (1887)
evidence.locator: Printed pp. 495–1017, especially main text pp. 495–776, appendix pp. 780–877, and corrections pp. 1010–1017
evidence.summary: Supports volume-II bibliographic data and the separation of main continuation, shorter-recension appendix, glossary/index outside the work boundary, and corrections apparatus.

evidence.citation: Wright, Metrical Chronicle, vol. I, Preface
evidence.locator: Preface, especially pp. xl–xlix, witness descriptions and editorial acknowledgments
evidence.summary: Names the eight collated sigla A/B/C/α/β/γ/δ/ε, identifies Cotton as base, records the Harley lacuna supply and direct rechecking, distinguishes two uncollated later copies, describes recension presentation, and credits E. Brock's working Cotton transcript.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata and scandata for metricalchronicl01robe and metricalchronicl02robe
evidence.locator: Both cached metadata JSON files and both scandata XML files
evidence.summary: Confirms two separate Internet Archive carrier identities, ARKs, source-page/PDF extents, file inventories, and scan metadata; it does not prove identity with the U-M page-image layer or supply an item-level open license for scan files.
notes: Volume-I metadata SHA-256 a61bccbfd3da78a66acadb0232e29eb7511b50f77a4026b4b455419251b90b62; scandata 37375540411f52ead83563cfab0353a06e3948d9fdb28ca277a177241144097a.
Volume-II metadata SHA-256 33303558ed05f7e04397111372a12459d48c96e79e28c09ef88c2837869bed9d; scandata c5e68ee7f9a2cf438205f6cb48dfddaa500798a4a52c072d9040a2275ce9b07c.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Caligula A XI, Chronicle component
evidence.summary: Confirms the exact physical Chronicle component, current last-quarter-thirteenth-century date, folio extent ff. 3r–13v and 15r–168v, imperfection, and repository identity.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 201
evidence.summary: Confirms the physical witness, current 1400–1424 date, repository identity, and Chronicle component at ff. 4r–161r.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 19677
evidence.summary: Confirms the fourteenth-century vellum witness, missing initial leaves, endpoint before the brief chronicle on f. 92v, and repository identity.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge Manuscripts Catalogue, R.4.26 (James 655)
evidence.locator: Physical description, contents, Chronicle ff. 2v–163r, and French Brut overlap
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, fourteenth-century parchment description, 170-leaf extent, dimensions, Chronicle range, and shared closing surfaces.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge IIIF manifest and audited complete provider-exposed bundle for R.4.26
evidence.locator: One sequence, 344 canvases: front cover, inside front board, four flyleaves recto/verso, ff. 1r–166v, inside back board, back cover; Chronicle canvases 14–335
evidence.summary: The ZIP contains manifest and exact-source inventory plus 344 unique 1800 × 2371 JPEGs. CRC, hashes, byte counts, URLs, canvas/service IDs, labels, and dimensions all validate. Completeness is bounded to the provider object; no spine or edge images are exposed.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge, Wren Digital Library: digital photography, image supply, and licensing
evidence.summary: Provides broader personal-study, image-supply, and publication-permission context. It is kept distinct from the exact R.4.26 manifest's CC BY-NC 4.0 item-level license.

evidence.citation: Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Digby 205
evidence.summary: Confirms the current fourteenth-century catalog date, parchment support, 124-leaf extent, Chronicle identity, repository, and shelfmark.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian one-canvas IIIF object for MS. Digby 205
evidence.summary: Confirms a complete one-canvas digital object containing the Chronicle opening and separate mid-fifteenth-century digital-object metadata. It is partial coverage of the 124-leaf manuscript and never evidence of a complete public Digby facsimile.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian Terms of Use
evidence.summary: Explains Digital Bodleian's common CC BY-NC 4.0 licensing, attribution, item-level restriction checks, and distinction between noncommercial and commercial reuse.

evidence.citation: A Catalogue of the Manuscripts Preserved in the Library of the University of Cambridge, vol. II (Cambridge, 1857), Ee.4.31
evidence.summary: Confirms 272 leaves, mixed paper/parchment support described as chiefly parchment, later-fifteenth-century catalog dating, and the abridged Chronicle at ff. 52r–199v. Wright's different vellum/date/hand account is preserved separately.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library Archives Access Policy
evidence.summary: Provides institutional mediated-access, handling, and restriction context; it does not contain the target text or establish a public complete facsimile or reproduction license.

evidence.citation: Senate House Library Archives Catalogue, MS 278
evidence.summary: Confirms that current MS 278 is the former Mostyn no. 259/H.7.c/old catalog 38, dates it to about 1440, describes 149 vellum leaves and the shorter Chronicle to 1272, and records current conservation closure.

evidence.citation: Magdalene College, Pepys Library catalogues, research tools, and reader guidance
evidence.summary: Provides current catalog research routes, supervised reader-access context, and permission/reproduction guidance for the Pepys Library. The cached guides are supporting evidence and contain no target text or images.
notes: Catalog-guide SHA-256 97f6bb5663499c883d38f7a479cb0a3daefc038985388af49270d715d279b285; reader-guide SHA-256 50155c686fd3582dbcd572a0b53f2a34a9a49ee00006e422fe03f54b881ea271.

evidence.citation: Magdalene College, Pepys Digital Library
evidence.summary: The current public digital selection was checked and did not include Pepys MS 2014; absence from a selection is not evidence that no private or repository surrogate exists.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge Archives, Add. MS c/172, Collation of the Manuscripts of Robert of Gloucester
evidence.summary: Identifies Wright's notebook as documenting collation of Cotton and Digby with readings from Trinity R.4.26 and Cambridge Ee.4.31. It supports edition process but is not a medieval textual ancestor.

evidence.citation: Matthew Fisher, Once Called Albion: The Composition and Transmission of History Writing in England, 1280–1350 (DPhil thesis, University of Oxford, 2005)
evidence.summary: Supports uncertain Robert attribution, internal dating of both recensions, Worcestershire–Gloucestershire linguistic region, witness transmission, Pepys fragment ranges, and the conceptual distinction between recensions and one Chronicle.

evidence.citation: Oxford University Research Archive record for Fisher, Once Called Albion
evidence.summary: Confirms thesis title, author, date, institutional deposit, and public download route; public access is not treated as an open reuse license.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright
evidence.locator: U.S. duration rules for works first published before 1929
evidence.summary: Supports the United States public-domain classification of Wright's 1887 publication. It contains no Chronicle text and does not determine non-U.S. status, image rights, provider contracts, or modern markup rights.

open_questions.question: Does the pinned CME repository's root Unlicense expressly cover inherited U-M XML data, transcription, encoding, and markup at CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/ahb1378.xml?
next_steps: Seek a path-specific licensing or provenance statement from the CME maintainers.
Until then, retain unknown repository-artifact status and do not transfer the software-worded root license to upstream textual, edition, image, or encoding layers.

open_questions.question: Can U-M provide the complete historical page-image inventory or bundle, identify the physical copies scanned, and document the exact OCR-to-image production chain for AHB1378.0001.001?
next_steps: Ask U-M for the complete image inventory, bundle, and production record.
Compare any supplied images against both independently modeled Internet Archive carriers before asserting copy identity or derivation.
Clarify whether the current No Copyright–United States statement applies separately to OCR/XML and page-image delivery despite the generic 2006 warning.

open_questions.question: What exact transformation pipeline, maintenance history, and license govern the Medictionary successor XML and its project-specific markup?
next_steps: Seek a project statement identifying the U-M source version, conversion rules, and dates.
Ask for a path-specific license covering XML markup and metadata.
Preserve the verified main-reading-stream identity and apparatus-level differences without treating the two serializations as byte-identical.

open_questions.question: Who composed and revised the two recensions, and how should the internally argued post-1297 or possible post-1307 longer-recension date be reconciled with Cotton Caligula A XI's current last-quarter-thirteenth-century catalog date?
next_steps: Review post-2005 scholarship on the Robert self-reference, recension chronology, and manuscript stemma.
Seek the British Library's current codicological basis for the Cotton component date.
Retain separate attributed claims rather than forcing one normalized date or a certain one-author model.

open_questions.question: Does E. Brock's working transcript of Cotton Caligula A XI survive, and where is it now held?
next_steps: Search Wright's surviving papers and Trinity archival descriptions for Brock's transcript.
Ask Trinity and the British Library whether a transcript matching Wright's acknowledgment is identifiable.
Keep the transcript as a documented process layer with unknown survival rather than inventing a present holding.

open_questions.question: Can complete, reusable, whole-provider-object or whole-physical-codex facsimiles be obtained for the six witnesses without verified complete public delivery, and can Digby 205 be imaged beyond the published f. 9r canvas?
next_steps: Ask the British Library first about existing complete surrogates for Cotton Caligula A XI, Harley 201, and Add 19677 before commissioning photography.
Ask Bodleian for an existing or new complete Digby 205 surrogate; state explicitly that the current public object contains only f. 9r.
Ask CUL, Senate House, and Magdalene about existing complete legacy or preservation surrogates, current conservation limits, and complete delivered image inventories.

For any new order, request covers, boards and interiors, spine, pastedowns, all endleaves/flyleaves and blanks, stubs, every leaf recto/verso, edges if offered, and an explicit omissions list; negotiate repository redistribution and publication terms separately.
notes: No enquiry or reproduction order has been submitted; these are planned mediated routes, not pending external correspondence.

open_questions.question: How should Trinity's exact R.4.26 CC BY-NC 4.0 manifest license be applied alongside the Wren Digital Library's broader request for publication permission?
next_steps: For intended publication or redistribution, cite the exact manifest license and ask Trinity whether any additional permission is expected.
Do not weaken the exact item-level license by inference or ignore the broader institutional guidance.

review_notes: The lineage graph counts one intellectual work in the paired metadata manifest while preserving eight medieval witness identities, two Wright volume expressions, and distinct digital carriers.
Mostyn no. 259 and Senate House MS 278 are the same physical witness.

Each witness-to-recension version_of edge is explicitly scoped to the Chronicle textual component and used only as the schema-native proxy for carries or attests; it does not make the physical codex itself an abstract textual version.

The Medictionary successor preserves an identical note-excluded normalized BODY reading stream but transforms apparatus and structural markup, so it is neither collapsed into nor treated as textually unrelated to the U-M encoding.
